headline: Chevron Leviathan Shutdown Tests Resilience Of CVX Valuation And Momentum
summary: Chevron's Leviathan gas field has been taken offline following an Israeli government order tied to escalating conflict with Iran. The shutdown is directly linked to recent US Israel strikes on Iran and subsequent retaliatory actions in the region. The disruption affects a major source of regional gas supply and a key upstream asset for Chevron (NYSE:CVX). For investors watching NYSE:CVX, this situation shows how geopolitics can suddenly affect a specific asset, not just the wider energy...

headline: Sintana Energy, Bluefield Solar Income Fund, Helix Exploration, Kodal Minerals - Small Cap Snapshot
summary: Sintana Energy Inc (TSX-V:SEI, OTCQB:SEUSF, FRA:3ZX1, AIM:SEI) has started a major 3D seismic survey on its AREA OFF-1 licence offshore Uruguay. The 4,300 square kilometre programme is backed by Chevron, with first results expected in late 2026. Bluefield Solar Income Fund Ltd (LSE:BSIF, FRA:5B3) reported a solid operational performance for the half year to end December, but a lower net asset value. The dividend is covered, the sale process is ongoing, and shares ended December at 68.5 pence. HeLIX Exploration PLC (AIM:HEX, OTCQB:HHEXF) has raised £2.2 million in a placing at 25 pence per share. The cash supports well work in Montana as the company moves further into helium production. Kodal Minerals PLC (AIM:KOD) has begun arbitration over a US$15 million payment to the Mali government. The dispute with joint venture partner Hainan Mining centres on whether that payment should be indemnified. Follow us and subscribe on YouTube, our social channels and on proactiveinvestors.co.uk.

content.title: Sintana Energy, Bluefield Solar Income Fund, Helix Exploration, Kodal Minerals - Small Cap Snapshot
content.description:
Sintana Energy Inc (TSX-V:SEI, OTCQB:SEUSF, FRA:3ZX1, AIM:SEI) has started a major 3D seismic survey on its AREA OFF-1 licence offshore Uruguay. The 4,300 square kilometre programme is backed by Chevron, with first results expected in late 2026.

Bluefield Solar Income Fund Ltd (LSE:BSIF, FRA:5B3) reported a solid operational performance for the half year to end December, but a lower net asset value. The dividend is covered, the sale process is ongoing, and shares ended December at 68.5 pence.

HeLIX Exploration PLC (AIM:HEX, OTCQB:HHEXF) has raised £2.2 million in a placing at 25 pence per share. The cash supports well work in Montana as the company moves further into helium production.

Kodal Minerals PLC (AIM:KOD) has begun arbitration over a US$15 million payment to the Mali government. The dispute with joint venture partner Hainan Mining centres on whether that payment should be indemnified.

Follow us and subscribe on YouTube, our social channels and on proactiveinvestors.co.uk.

The 4,300 square kilometre programme is backed by Chevron, with first results expected in late 2026. Bluefield Solar Income Fund Ltd (LSE:BSIF, FRA:5B3) reported a solid operational performance for the half year to end December, but a lower net asset value. The dividend is covered, the sale process is ongoing, and shares ended December at 68.5 pence. HeLIX Exploration PLC (AIM:HEX, OTCQB:HHEXF) has raised £2.2 million in a placing at 25 pence per share. The cash supports well work in Montana as the company moves further into helium production. Kodal Minerals PLC (AIM:KOD) has begun arbitration over a US$15 million payment to the Mali government. headline: Cheniere, Exxon, Chevron Stocks Rise Again. The Energy Shock Is Upending Markets.
summary: Energy stocks were surging ahead of the bell Tuesday, with oil and gas prices soaring as fighting in the Middle East showed no signs of slowing.  Shares in oil exploration and production companies rallied again ahead of the bell—  ConocoPhillips  and  Occidental Petroleum  were both up by more than 3%.  Exxon Mobil  and  Chevron  were also higher.

content.title: Cheniere, Exxon, Chevron Stocks Rise Again. The Energy Shock Is Upending Markets.
content.summary: Energy stocks were surging ahead of the bell Tuesday, with oil and gas prices soaring as fighting in the Middle East showed no signs of slowing.  Shares in oil exploration and production companies rallied again ahead of the bell—  ConocoPhillips  and  Occidental Petroleum  were both up by more than 3%.  Exxon Mobil  and  Chevron  were also higher.

headline: Oil Prices Top $80 a Barrel. But Natural Gas Is a Big Problem for Markets.
summary: International oil benchmarks rose early Tuesday, extending gains, amid escalating tensions in the Middle East.  Brent crude futures  were trading 6.1% higher, at $82.48 per barrel.  West Texas Intermediate  futures were also rising – up by 6.1% to $75.61.

headline: What Chevron (CVX)'s Leviathan Shutdown Amid Middle East Tensions Means For Shareholders
summary: In recent days, Chevron has halted production and declared force majeure at its Leviathan natural gas field in Israel amid escalating U.S.–Israel–Iran hostilities and disruptions near the Strait of Hormuz that have tightened global oil and gas supplies. These events highlight how Chevron’s direct exposure to Middle Eastern infrastructure and its broader upstream portfolio can both face operational risk and benefit from higher commodity prices when geopolitical tensions affect energy...

headline: Oil Prices Soar to $83 a Barrel. A Potential Energy Shock Is Brewing.
summary: Oil prices climbed higher on Tuesday, extending Monday’s gains as fighting in the Middle East escalated.  Brent crude oil futures  were trading 7.4% higher, at $83.51 a barrel.  West Texas Intermediate  futures were up 6.8% to $76.09.

headline: Reddit Is Bullish on XLE But Crude Oil Tells a Different Story
summary: The State Street Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLE), is up 27% year-to-date and 13% over the past month, trading at $56.78 Monday morning. The catalyst is unmistakable: Iran-Israel tensions have escalated sharply, with the U.S. ordering non-essential embassy staff out of Israel, a development the world is keeping a close eye on.
